message:
chore(beads): Sync issue tracking state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -143,7 +143,7 @@ {"id":"bd-2x0.7","title":"Implement JSON tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement JSON tokenizer ## Context JSON is ubiquitous for configuration and data exchange. It's also one of the simplest languages to tokenize, making it a good starting point and reference for testing the highlighting system. ## JSON Syntax (Complete Specification) JSON has exactly these token types: ### Structural Characters ``` { } [ ] : , ``` ### Literals ```json "string" // Strings (always double-quoted) 123 // Numbers (integer) -456 // Negative numbers 3.14 // Decimal numbers 1e10 // Scientific notation true // Boolean false // Boolean null // Null ``` ### String Escapes ```json \" \\ \/ \b \f \n \r \t \uXXXX ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/json.rs`: ```rust pub struct JsonTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for JsonTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "JSON" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["json", "jsonc", "json5"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // JSON is line-based for most purposes // Strings technically can't span lines in strict JSON // But we handle JSONC (with comments) and JSON5 (with multi-line) } } ``` ### Token Mapping | JSON Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `"key":` (object key) | Identifier or String | | `"value"` | String | | `\n`, `\t`, etc. | StringEscape | | `123`, `-3.14` | Number | | `true`, `false` | Boolean | | `null` | Constant | | `{`, `}`, `[`, `]` | Punctuation | | `:`, `,` | Delimiter | | `//` comment (JSONC) | Comment | | `/* */` comment (JSONC) | CommentBlock | ## Edge Cases - Distinguishing object keys from string values (keys are followed by `:`) - Unicode escapes (`\uXXXX`) - Scientific notation (`1e-10`) - JSONC comment support - Invalid JSON (highlight as Error) ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_json_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_booleans_null() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_structure() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_escapes() { ... } #[test] fn test_jsonc_comments() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/json.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All JSON value types highlighted correctly - [ ] Object keys distinguished from string values - [ ] String escapes highlighted - [ ] Numbers with all formats (int, float, scientific, negative) - [ ] JSONC comment support - [ ] Invalid syntax highlighted as Error - [ ] Test coverage >90% (JSON is simple enough for high coverage)","notes":"## Performance Requirements Same as all tokenizers (per AGENTS.md): - **Zero allocations per token** - Use &str slices into source - **<1μs per line** for typical JSON - JSON is simpler than Rust, so performance should be even better ## Testing with Tracing Per bd-2x0.11: - All tests use tracing (info!, debug!) - Run: RUST_LOG=debug cargo test json_tokenizer -- --nocapture ## Note on JSONC Support JSONC (JSON with Comments) is common in VS Code configs. Support single-line `//` and block `/* */` comments, but mark the file extension separately (.jsonc) to not confuse strict JSON parsers.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:17.329963585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051156990Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051138806Z","close_reason":"Implemented JSON tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.8","title":"Implement TOML tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement TOML tokenizer ## Context TOML (Tom's Obvious Minimal Language) is the standard configuration format for Rust projects (Cargo.toml). Since OpenTUI is a Rust library, excellent TOML support is particularly relevant. ## TOML Syntax Elements ### Structure ```toml [section] [section.subsection] [[array.of.tables]] key = value "quoted key" = value dotted.key = value ``` ### Values ```toml # Strings string = "basic" multiline = """ multi line """ literal = 'no \escapes' literal_multiline = ''' multi line ''' # Numbers integer = 42 hex = 0xDEADBEEF octal = 0o755 binary = 0b11010110 float = 3.14 infinity = inf nan = nan # Boolean bool = true # Dates and times date = 2024-01-15 time = 14:30:00 datetime = 2024-01-15T14:30:00Z datetime_local = 2024-01-15T14:30:00 # Arrays array = [1, 2, 3] nested = [[1, 2], [3, 4]] # Inline tables inline = { key = "value", other = 42 } ``` ### Comments ```toml # Single line comment only key = "value" # End of line comment ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs`: ```rust pub struct TomlTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for TomlTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "TOML" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["toml"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle multi-line string continuation // Section headers [name] // Key = value pairs // Comments } } ``` ### Token Mapping | TOML Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `[section]` | Type | | `[[array]]` | Type | | `key =` | Identifier | | `"string"` | String | | `42`, `0xFF` | Number | | `true`/`false` | Boolean | | `2024-01-15` | Number (or custom Date) | | `# comment` | Comment | | `=`, `,` | Operator | | `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling - Multi-line basic strings: `""" ... """` - Multi-line literal strings: `''' ... '''` ## Edge Cases - Dotted keys (`package.version`) - Bare keys vs quoted keys - Date/time literals (many formats) - Unicode in strings and keys - Inline tables vs regular tables ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_toml_sections() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_key_values() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_multiline_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_dates() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_arrays() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_inline_tables() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Section headers `[name]` highlighted as Type - [ ] Keys highlighted as Identifier - [ ] All string variants (basic, literal, multiline) - [ ] Numbers including special formats (hex, date/time) - [ ] Comments highlighted - [ ] Multi-line strings work correctly - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization - return spans not owned strings. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. TOML is critical for Cargo.toml editing use cases.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:50.867576492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748398045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748379901Z","close_reason":"Implemented TOML tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.9","title":"Implement Markdown tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement Markdown tokenizer ## Context Markdown is the de facto standard for documentation, READMEs, and notes. Terminal-based editors and tools frequently work with Markdown, making it a high-value tokenizer to include. ## Markdown Syntax Elements ### Headings ```markdown # Heading 1 ## Heading 2 ### Heading 3 (up to 6) Alternative Heading ================== Alternative H2 -------------- ``` ### Emphasis ```markdown *italic* or _italic_ **bold** or __bold__ ***bold italic*** ~~strikethrough~~ ``` ### Code ```markdown `inline code` indented code block (4 spaces) ```language fenced code block ``` ``` ### Links and Images ```markdown [link text](url) [link text](url "title") [reference link][ref] [ref]: url ![alt text](image.png) ![alt text](image.png "title") ``` ### Lists ```markdown - Unordered item * Also unordered + Also unordered 1. Ordered item 2. Ordered item - [ ] Task item unchecked - [x] Task item checked ``` ### Blockquotes ```markdown > Quote line > Continued quote ``` ### Horizontal Rules ```markdown --- *** ___ ``` ### Tables (GFM) ```markdown | Header | Header | |--------|--------| | Cell | Cell | ``` ### HTML ```markdown
Raw HTML
``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs`: ```rust pub struct MarkdownTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for MarkdownTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "Markdown" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["md", "markdown", "mkd", "mkdn"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle fenced code block continuation // Line-start elements: headings, lists, blockquotes, HR // Inline elements: emphasis, code, links } } ``` ### Token Mapping | Markdown Element | TokenKind | |-----------------|-----------| | `# Heading` | Heading | | `*italic*` | Emphasis | | `**bold**` | Emphasis + Bold style | | `[text](url)` | Link | | `![alt](url)` | Link | | `` `code` `` | CodeInline | | ``` ```code``` ``` | CodeBlock | | `> quote` | Comment (or custom Quote) | | `- item` | Punctuation + Text | | `---` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling 1. **Fenced code blocks** - ``` ... ``` (track the fence marker) 2. **Indented code blocks** - 4+ spaces at line start 3. **Multi-line blockquotes** - Lines starting with `>` ## Edge Cases - Escaping: `\*not italic\*` - Nested emphasis: `***bold and italic***` - Links with parentheses in URL - Code spans with backticks inside: ``` `` `code` `` ``` - Setext headings (underline style) - HTML blocks ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_md_headings() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_emphasis() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_inline() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_blocks() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_links() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_lists() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_blockquotes() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_escaping() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Headings (ATX and Setext styles) - [ ] Emphasis (italic, bold, strikethrough) - [ ] Inline and fenced code - [ ] Links and images - [ ] Lists (ordered, unordered, task) - [ ] Blockquotes - [ ] Fenced code block continuation - [ ] Escape sequences - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. Markdown is high-value for documentation editing in terminal apps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:46:23.879677476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704966007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704947462Z","close_reason":"Implemented Markdown tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} -{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570602896Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:08.399425192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds) ## Purpose The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains: - how to run the demo - what it demonstrates - essential keybinds - tour mode and headless smoke mode - recommended terminals for best visuals ## Content Requirements Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`: Run: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase Tour (unattended): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour Headless smoke: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json Degradation matrix (example manual run): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results. ## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv) Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category): Interactive: - --fps - --tour - --exit-after-tour - --max-frames Headless/testing: - --headless-smoke - --headless-dump-json - --headless-check - --headless-size Terminal behavior: - --no-mouse - --no-alt-screen - --no-cap-queries Capability override: - --cap-preset Advanced: - --threaded - --seed ## Acceptance Notes - README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it. - Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:03.087136609Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade) ## Purpose Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show: - translucent overlays (opacity stack) - animated alpha fades (time-driven) - crisp typography + spacing They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur. ## Behaviors - Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar). - Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap. - Each toast has: - severity: Info / Warn / Error - title + optional detail line - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry) ## Rendering Notes - Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border. - Severity affects accent color and icon glyph. - Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar. ## Integration Points Toasts should be produced by: - command palette executions - tour step transitions - simulated preview errors/retries - clipboard/link actions Implementation suggestion: - `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }` - `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque }` - render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203757246Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -161,10 +161,10 @@ {"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`) ## Purpose OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode. This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications. ## Behavior - CLI flag: `--threaded` - When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`. - The UI + state machine should behave the same. ## Design Constraint `Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app: - Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper: - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }` - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()` ## GraphemePool Note (Important) If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs: - Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool. - Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns. - If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly. ## Acceptance Notes - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly. - Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962836338Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3pe","title":"demo_showcase UI: help overlay (F1) + feature legend","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Help Overlay (F1) + Feature Legend ## Purpose The help overlay makes the demo self-contained and also proves multiple library features: - overlay rendering + opacity stack (glass modal) - scissor clipping (scrollable help content) - hyperlink rendering via OSC 8 (LinkPool) It also prevents the “what do I press?” problem for first-time viewers. ## Content Requirements ### Keybinds Include (at minimum): - `Ctrl+Q` quit - `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` focus next/prev panel - `Ctrl+P` command palette - `F1` help - `Ctrl+T` tour start/stop - editor keys (arrows, word movement, wrap toggle, undo/redo) - log/preview navigation keys ### Mouse - click to focus panels - click to activate buttons - scroll to scroll lists/logs ### Feature Legend Make it explicit where each OpenTUI feature is visible in the app: - RGBA alpha blending (preview overlays / glass modals) - scissor stack (sidebar/log scroll clipping) - opacity stack (overlays + tinted UI sections) - grapheme pool (unicode panel) - OSC 8 links (logs + help) - hitgrid (clickable UI) - pixel buffers (preview panel) - diff rendering + sync output (perf/inspector + smoothness) ### Clickable Links Include a small “Docs / Links” section with OSC 8 hyperlinks: - repo URL - unicode width reference The links must be allocated through `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link`. ## Rendering Notes - Help content should be scrollable when terminal is small. - Use clear typography hierarchy: title, section headers, monospace keycaps. - Use hit areas for the “Close” button and for any tab-like subviews.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.642163943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666034752Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:04.565909821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666002312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.092958035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.656207314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3vjm","title":"demo_showcase runtime: deterministic termination controls (--max-frames / --exit-after-tour)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Deterministic Termination Controls ## Purpose We need deterministic ways to stop the app without human input so we can: - run real PTY-based E2E tests without brittle "send keys" logic - record unattended videos reliably (tour ends -> app exits) - keep runs bounded for CI and debugging ## Required Controls ### 1) `--max-frames ` (Hard Frame Budget) - After the demo has **presented N frames**, exit 0. - Count frames consistently across modes (interactive + headless). - Print a stable summary line on exit (useful for tests/logs), e.g.: - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames= last_dirty_cells=` ### 2) `--exit-after-tour` (Tour Completion Exit) - When tour mode reaches the last step and completes it, exit 0 automatically. - The exit should be clean: - restore cursor - leave alt screen (if used) - disable mouse (if enabled) - Tour should still be escapable with user input; this flag only adds an automatic exit at completion. ## Integration Notes - Flag parsing belongs in `bd-2iv`, but the **run-loop semantics** and **tour end handling** live here. - Headless mode (`bd-2el`) should also honor these controls so tests can bound runtime. ## Why This Matters Without deterministic termination, we end up with flaky tests and awkward demo workflows. These controls make the demo feel "professional" to develop and validate.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `--max-frames ` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode and in headless smoke mode. - `--exit-after-tour` exits 0 when the final tour step completes (both interactive and headless tour runs). - Exits are clean: cursor restored, mouse disabled (if enabled), and alt screen exited (if used). - A stable summary line is printed on exit for deterministic debugging/logging. - PTY E2E tests can rely on these controls to avoid sending synthetic keypresses.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.678047128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:26.318650041Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.707235213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.718517533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.723946582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.712843797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default) ## Purpose Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior. This bead adds true end-to-end tests by running the demo under a pseudo-terminal (PTY), capturing ANSI output bytes, and asserting on key invariants: - alternate screen entry/exit - cursor hide/show - mouse enable sequences (if enabled) - synchronized output enable (if supported) - OSC 8 hyperlink sequences appear (and do NOT appear when disabled) ## Approach (Unix) - Use libc openpty / fork+exec (or an equivalent minimal PTY approach) to spawn: - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames 600 --fps 30 Why both exit controls? - `--exit-after-tour` is the intended deterministic terminator. - `--max-frames` is a hard safety bound so the PTY test cannot hang if tour logic regresses. - Capture stdout from the PTY master. ## Assertions Assert the output stream contains, in some order: - enter alt screen (CSI ? 1049 h) when alt screen enabled - leave alt screen (CSI ? 1049 l) on exit - hide cursor (CSI ? 25 l) and show cursor (CSI ? 25 h) - mouse enable sequences (1000/1002/1006) when mouse enabled - synchronized output enable (CSI ? 2026 h) when supported/enabled Hyperlink coverage: - run one case with default settings and assert at least one OSC 8 sequence appears (ESC ] 8 ; ; ...) - run one case with `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` (or equivalent disable) and assert OSC 8 sequences are absent Optional (high-value): threaded mode parity - run a second spawn with `--threaded` and assert the same terminal lifecycle sequences appear Also assert: - demo exits 0 within a timeout - output is non-trivial (bytes > some threshold) ## Logging / Artifacts - Use the structured logger utilities in `tests/common/harness.rs` to: - log key milestones (spawned, bytes captured, exit status) - write captured ANSI bytes to artifacts (both raw + hex/escaped view) Controlled via HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1. ## Test Placement - Mark PTY tests as #[ignore] by default to avoid CI flakiness. - Provide a script (separate bead) to run them locally and collect artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.613268330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:33.389722159Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.862668126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.631306455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:28.902157577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:19.691230196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:26.694765952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default) ## Purpose Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior. Could pre-allocate with estimated capacity based on typical change rate, or use a reusable scratch buffer.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:54:52.939084594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:53.872340399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:53.871467724Z","close_reason":"Pre-allocated changed_cells Vec based on total size heuristic; cargo check/clippy/fmt clean","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-bb2","title":"Fix clippy pedantic lints in tests and source files","description":"68 clippy errors found. Mostly similar_names, many_single_char_names, uninlined_format_args, missing_const_for_fn, semicolon_if_nothing_returned, format_collect lints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:09:28.502462752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:06:34.124269151Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:06:34.123780311Z","close_reason":"Clippy/Check/Fmt clean; no warnings with cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} -{"id":"bd-bqd1","title":"demo_showcase tests: tour determinism regression (scripted steps -> stable output)","description":"# demo_showcase Tests — Tour Determinism Regression (Scripted Steps → Stable Output) ## Purpose Tour mode is a flagship feature and also our best deterministic “integration test” for: - input routing - overlays - panel switching - pixel/alpha/unicode/link features This bead adds regression tests that ensure the tour produces stable, deterministic outputs. ## Strategy Run the demo in headless mode and execute the tour script (no real TTY). Recommended CLI shape (keep it strict): - --headless-smoke --tour --headless-dump-json Optional safety bound (should not be needed if tour completes, but prevents hangs): - --max-frames The JSON output must include: - step index + step title - per-frame (or per-step) dirty cell counts - sentinel markers proving that step’s feature is active ## What To Assert For each tour step (from `bd-1gy`): - the expected step title appears in the JSON - the expected feature markers are present, e.g.: - palette open marker on the command palette step - unicode strings (ZWJ emoji) on the grapheme pool step - at least one OSC8 link marker on the hyperlinks step - pixel preview active marker on the pixel step Additionally: - tour output is deterministic across runs (snapshot the JSON via insta or compare stable hashes) - tour terminates cleanly (headless tour run exits 0) ## Logging - Print a clear reproduction command on failure. - Store JSON and logs as artifacts when HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1. ## Acceptance Notes - A CI test fails loudly if tour output changes unexpectedly. - The logged diff is sufficient to diagnose what changed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.832960799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:28.496790059Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-2el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:53.529196779Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.852222684Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:52.933298797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-bqd1","title":"demo_showcase tests: tour determinism regression (scripted steps -> stable output)","description":"# demo_showcase Tests — Tour Determinism Regression (Scripted Steps → Stable Output) ## Purpose Tour mode is a flagship feature and also our best deterministic “integration test” for: - Recommended CLI shape (keep it strict): - --headless-smoke --tour --headless-dump-json Optional safety bound (should not be needed if tour completes, but prevents hangs): - --max-frames The JSON output must include: - step index + step title - per-frame (or per-step) dirty cell counts - sentinel markers proving that step’s feature is active ## What To Assert For each tour step (from `bd-1gy`): - the expected step title appears in the JSON - the expected feature markers are present, e.g.: - palette open marker on the command palette step - unicode strings (ZWJ emoji) on the grapheme pool step - at least one OSC8 link marker on the hyperlinks step - pixel preview active marker on the pixel step Additionally: - tour output is deterministic across runs (snapshot the JSON via insta or compare stable hashes) - tour terminates cleanly (headless tour run exits 0) ## Logging - Print a clear reproduction command on failure. - Store JSON and logs as artifacts when HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1. ## Acceptance Notes - A CI test fails loudly if tour output changes unexpectedly. - The logged diff is sufficient to diagnose what changed.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - A CI-friendly test runs `demo_showcase` headlessly with `--tour --headless-dump-json` and exits 0. - The resulting JSON includes step markers and feature markers per step. - The test asserts determinism via insta snapshots (or stable hashes) across runs. - On failure, logs include reproduction commands and artifacts are stored when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.832960799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:00.579873891Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-2el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:53.529196779Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.852222684Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:52.933298797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-c75","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: UI layout + components (dashboard/editor/preview/logs)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — UI Layout + Components ## Purpose Build the actual “app” the user sees. The demo should present a believable multi-panel workflow: - Top bar (branding, time, mode, quick actions) - Sidebar navigation (projects / sections) - Main editor panel (real editing + highlighting) - Preview panel (pixel/graphics + charts) - Logs/notifications panel (events + hyperlinks) - Command palette overlay + help overlay All of these must strongly exercise OpenTUI primitives: scissor, opacity, blending, styled text, pooling.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist: - [ ] Layout feels like a real modern TUI app - [ ] Every panel is clipped correctly (scissor) - [ ] Overlays feel “glassy” (opacity + blending) - [ ] Editor is fully interactive and highlighted - [ ] Preview panel demonstrates pixel rendering and compositing - [ ] Logs panel demonstrates hyperlinks and event visualization","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:17.719974057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:26.625591657Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","epic","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c75","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:17.742038951Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-gtp","title":"Add Line Duplication and Movement Methods","description":"## Overview Add line duplication and movement methods to EditBuffer. ## Already Implemented (NO WORK NEEDED) The following methods already exist in `src/text/edit.rs`: - `delete_line()` at line 381 ✓ - `goto_line()` at line 292 ✓ ## Scope (Methods to Add) ```rust impl EditBuffer { /// Duplicate the current line (insert copy below). pub fn duplicate_line(&mut self) { let row = self.cursor.row; if let Some(line) = self.buffer.rope().line(row) { let line_text = line.to_string(); let line_end = self.buffer.rope().line_to_char(row) + line.len_chars(); // Insert newline + line content after current line let insert_pos = if line_text.ends_with('\n') { line_end } else { // At last line without trailing newline line_end }; self.begin_edit_group(); if !line_text.ends_with('\n') { self.insert_at(insert_pos, "\n"); } self.insert_at(insert_pos + if line_text.ends_with('\n') { 0 } else { 1 }, &line_text); self.end_edit_group(); } } /// Move current line up (swap with line above). pub fn move_line_up(&mut self) { if self.cursor.row == 0 { return; // Can't move first line up } let current_row = self.cursor.row; let prev_row = current_row - 1; self.begin_edit_group(); // Get both lines let current_line = self.buffer.rope().line(current_row).map(|l| l.to_string()); let prev_line = self.buffer.rope().line(prev_row).map(|l| l.to_string()); if let (Some(curr), Some(prev)) = (current_line, prev_line) { // Delete both lines and reinsert in swapped order let start = self.buffer.rope().line_to_char(prev_row); let end = self.buffer.rope().line_to_char(current_row) + self.buffer.rope().line(current_row).map(|l| l.len_chars()).unwrap_or(0); self.delete_range_offsets(start, end); self.insert_at(start, &format!("{}{}", curr, prev)); // Move cursor to new position (one line up) self.goto_line(prev_row); } self.end_edit_group(); } /// Move current line down (swap with line below). pub fn move_line_down(&mut self) { let current_row = self.cursor.row; let next_row = current_row + 1; if next_row >= self.buffer.len_lines() { return; // Can't move last line down } self.begin_edit_group(); // Similar to move_line_up but swap in opposite direction let current_line = self.buffer.rope().line(current_row).map(|l| l.to_string()); let next_line = self.buffer.rope().line(next_row).map(|l| l.to_string()); if let (Some(curr), Some(next)) = (current_line, next_line) { let start = self.buffer.rope().line_to_char(current_row); let end = self.buffer.rope().line_to_char(next_row) + self.buffer.rope().line(next_row).map(|l| l.len_chars()).unwrap_or(0); self.delete_range_offsets(start, end); self.insert_at(start, &format!("{}{}", next, curr)); // Move cursor to new position (one line down) self.goto_line(next_row); } self.end_edit_group(); } } ``` ## Files to Modify - src/text/edit.rs - Add the 3 new methods ## Testing Requirements ### Unit Tests (add to existing tests in src/text/edit.rs) ```rust #[test] fn test_duplicate_line() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("line 1\nline 2\nline 3"); buffer.goto_line(1); // Move to "line 2" eprintln!("[TEST] Initial text:\n{}", buffer.text()); eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor on line {}", buffer.cursor().row); buffer.duplicate_line(); eprintln!("[TEST] After duplicate_line:\n{}", buffer.text()); assert_eq!(buffer.len_lines(), 4, "Should have 4 lines now"); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); eprintln!("[TEST] Lines: {:?}", lines); assert_eq!(lines[1], "line 2"); assert_eq!(lines[2], "line 2", "Duplicated line should match"); // Test undo buffer.undo(); assert_eq!(buffer.len_lines(), 3); eprintln!("[TEST] After undo: {} lines", buffer.len_lines()); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: duplicate_line works with undo"); } #[test] fn test_duplicate_last_line_no_newline() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("line 1\nline 2"); // No trailing newline buffer.goto_line(1); // Last line eprintln!("[TEST] Text (no trailing newline): {:?}", buffer.text()); buffer.duplicate_line(); eprintln!("[TEST] After duplicate: {:?}", buffer.text()); assert_eq!(buffer.len_lines(), 3); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[1], "line 2"); assert_eq!(lines[2], "line 2"); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: duplicate_line handles last line without newline"); } #[test] fn test_move_line_up() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("line 1\nline 2\nline 3"); buffer.goto_line(1); // "line 2" eprintln!("[TEST] Initial text:\n{}", buffer.text()); eprintln!("[TEST] Moving line 2 up"); buffer.move_line_up(); eprintln!("[TEST] After move_line_up:\n{}", buffer.text()); eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor now on line {}", buffer.cursor().row); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[0], "line 2", "Line 2 should be first now"); assert_eq!(lines[1], "line 1", "Line 1 should be second now"); assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().row, 0, "Cursor should follow line up"); // Test undo buffer.undo(); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[0], "line 1"); eprintln!("[TEST] After undo: first line is {:?}", lines[0]); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: move_line_up works with undo"); } #[test] fn test_move_line_down() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("line 1\nline 2\nline 3"); buffer.goto_line(1); // "line 2" eprintln!("[TEST] Initial text:\n{}", buffer.text()); eprintln!("[TEST] Moving line 2 down"); buffer.move_line_down(); eprintln!("[TEST] After move_line_down:\n{}", buffer.text()); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[1], "line 3", "Line 3 should be second now"); assert_eq!(lines[2], "line 2", "Line 2 should be third now"); assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().row, 2, "Cursor should follow line down"); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: move_line_down works"); } #[test] fn test_move_line_at_boundaries() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("first\nmiddle\nlast"); // Move first line up - should be no-op buffer.goto_line(0); buffer.move_line_up(); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[0], "first", "First line can't move up"); eprintln!("[TEST] move_line_up on first line is no-op"); // Move last line down - should be no-op buffer.goto_line(2); buffer.move_line_down(); let lines: Vec<&str> = buffer.text().lines().collect(); assert_eq!(lines[2], "last", "Last line can't move down"); eprintln!("[TEST] move_line_down on last line is no-op"); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Line move operations handle boundaries"); } #[test] fn test_line_operations_preserve_cursor_column() { let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("short\nthis is a longer line\nend"); buffer.goto_line(1); buffer.move_to(1, 10); // Middle of "longer line" eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor at row={} col={}", buffer.cursor().row, buffer.cursor().col); buffer.duplicate_line(); // Cursor should stay on original line eprintln!("[TEST] After duplicate: row={} col={}", buffer.cursor().row, buffer.cursor().col); eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Line operations preserve cursor column"); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `duplicate_line()` inserts copy of current line below - [ ] `duplicate_line()` handles last line without trailing newline - [ ] `move_line_up()` swaps current line with line above - [ ] `move_line_down()` swaps current line with line below - [ ] Move operations are no-ops at buffer boundaries - [ ] Cursor follows the moved/duplicated line - [ ] All operations integrate with undo/redo - [ ] All 6 new unit tests pass with detailed logging - [ ] Existing EditBuffer tests still pass (no regression)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] delete_line removes line and newline - [ ] goto_line moves cursor correctly - [ ] goto_line clamps to valid range - [ ] Line operations integrate with undo - [ ] All 8+ unit tests pass ---","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:52.119462179Z","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:08:22.545647283Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:08:22.545599323Z","close_reason":"Implemented duplicate_line(), move_line_up(), move_line_down() with comprehensive tests. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-hc0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: content + initial state wiring (editor/logs/metrics/nav)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Content + Initial State Wiring (Editor / Logs / Metrics / Nav) ## Purpose The demo should boot into a believable “project workspace” with rich content immediately visible. This bead wires the content pack spec (`bd-1ei`) into real runtime state: - initial editor buffers - initial sidebar navigation structure - initial logs stream - deterministic metrics sources ## What To Build Inside the demo binary, define a single source of truth for demo content: - `DemoContent` struct: - `files: Vec` - `links: DemoLinks { repo_url, docs_url, ... }` - `seed_logs: Vec` - `metric_params: MetricParams` And a single initializer: - `fn App::new(content: DemoContent, ...) -> App` ## Design Notes - The editor’s initial file should be Rust and highlight well with the built-in tokenizer registry. - The preview panel should have data available immediately (no empty state). - Logs should start with a small backlog so scrolling is demonstrable. - Metrics should come from deterministic functions (sin waves, ramps) so tour mode is reproducible. ## Acceptance Notes - Launching the demo immediately looks “alive” (content everywhere) without needing user actions. - The same run on the same terminal size produces the same initial content.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:34.644052627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:00:34.119219042Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hc0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:10.064578696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hc0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:34.662342935Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hc0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:00:34.119189096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
